title: Mario Ferrari

infobox.value: (September 3, 1894 – June 28, 1974) was an Italian [film actor](/wiki/Film_actor "Film actor"). After making his debut in 1920, Ferrari became a mainstay of Italian cinema during the [Fascist era](/wiki/Fascist_era "Fascist era") appearing in a mixture of leading and supporting roles. He played the villainous Graiano d'Asti in the historical film *[Ettore Fieramosca](/wiki/Ettore_Fieramosca_(1938_film) "Ettore Fieramosca (1938 film)")* (1938).[[1]](#cite_note-1) Ferrari continued to work regularly in the post-[Second World War](/wiki/Second_World_War "Second World War") years.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Italian actor (1894–1974)

| Mario Ferrari | |
| --- | --- |
| Ferrari in *[Ettore Fieramosca](/wiki/Ettore_Fieramosca_(1938_film) "Ettore Fieramosca (1938 film)")* (1938) | |
| Born | (1894-09-03)September 3, 1894  [Rome](/wiki/Rome "Rome"), [Lazio](/wiki/Lazio "Lazio")   [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy") |
| Died | June 28, 1974(1974-06-28) (aged 79)  Rome, Lazio   Italy |
| Occupation | Actor |
| Years active | 1920–1974 |

**Mario Ferrari** (September 3, 1894 – June 28, 1974) was an Italian [film actor](/wiki/Film_actor "Film actor"). After making his debut in 1920, Ferrari became a mainstay of Italian cinema during the [Fascist era](/wiki/Fascist_era "Fascist era") appearing in a mixture of leading and supporting roles. He played the villainous Graiano d'Asti in the historical film *[Ettore Fieramosca](/wiki/Ettore_Fieramosca_(1938_film) "Ettore Fieramosca (1938 film)")* (1938).[[1]](#cite_note-1) Ferrari continued to work regularly in the post-[Second World War](/wiki/Second_World_War "Second World War") years.
